title: RV8GR instruction latch
purpose: Latch the two fetched instruction bytes so control and operand signals stay stable during execute.

chips.role: IR_HIGH control byte latch, clocked by T0
IR_LOW operand byte latch, clocked by T1

ports.name: T2
ports.description: Execute phase; both latches must hold.

ports.name: IR_HIGH0..7
ports.description: U5 outputs: bit7 SUB, bit6 XOR_MODE, bit5 MUX_SEL, bit4 AC_WR, bit3 SRC, bit2 STR, bit1 BR, bit0 JMP.

ports.name: IRL0..IRL7
ports.description: U6 operand outputs to PC load, address mux, and U34 immediate buffer.

behavior.u5_capture: U5 captures IBUS on the rising edge of T0 only.
behavior.u6_capture: U6 captures IBUS on the rising edge of T1 only.
behavior.hold: Both latches hold through T2 and through phases where their own clock does not rise.
behavior.output_policy: IR_HIGH and IRL are each driven by one active 74HC574 output bank; U34, not U6, is the only device allowed to place IRL onto IBUS.
behavior.output_enable: U5 and U6 /OE pins are tied LOW; U34, not U6, controls when IRL drives IBUS.

timing.non_trigger_edge: No rising edge on the owning phase clock holds state.
timing.setup_hold_boundary: IBUS must settle before the T0/T1 rising edge and hold after it; physical R/C delay and setup/hold margin are covered by timing_margins.json, not claimed by this latch package alone.

guarantees: control-bit labels match the direct-control encoding

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
